react.answer:
## Building a Color Picker in React

**Mental model:** A color picker keeps one source of truth — RGB channels — and *derives* the hex string from them. Sliders edit channels; preset swatches set all three at once; the hex is computed for display/copy. Keep it controlled so the parent owns the value.

### Step 1 — RGB as the single source of truth
```jsx
const [rgb, setRgb] = useState({ r: 37, g: 99, b: 235 });
```

### Step 2 — Derive hex from RGB
Pad each channel to two hex digits.

```jsx
const toHex = ({ r, g, b }) => '#' + [r, g, b].map((c) => c.toString(16).padStart(2, '0')).join('');
```

### Step 3 — Channel sliders
Each slider updates one channel immutably.

```jsx
<input type="range" min="0" max="255" value={rgb.r}
  onChange={(e) => setRgb((c) => ({ ...c, r: +e.target.value }))} />
```

### Step 4 — Preset swatches
Parse a hex preset back into channels.

```jsx
const fromHex = (h) => ({ r: parseInt(h.slice(1, 3), 16), g: parseInt(h.slice(3, 5), 16), b: parseInt(h.slice(5, 7), 16) });
```

**Edge cases**

| Case | Handling |
| :-- | :-- |
| Single-digit channel | `padStart(2, '0')` keeps hex valid. |
| Invalid hex input | Validate with a regex before parsing. |
| Contrast of label | Pick black/white text based on luminance. |

**Interview tip:** Keeping RGB as the source and deriving hex (not storing both) avoids the classic two-fields-out-of-sync bug — mention you'd add HSL the same way: one canonical model, derived representations.

vue.answer:
## Building a Color Picker in Vue 3

**Mental model:** Keep one source of truth — RGB channels in a `reactive` object — and *derive* the hex string with a `computed`. Sliders edit channels; preset swatches set all three; hex is for display/copy.

### Step 1 — RGB source of truth
```vue
<script setup>
import { reactive, computed } from 'vue';
const rgb = reactive({ r: 37, g: 99, b: 235 });
</script>
```

### Step 2 — Derive hex with computed
```js
const hex = computed(() => '#' + [rgb.r, rgb.g, rgb.b].map((c) => c.toString(16).padStart(2, '0')).join(''));
```

### Step 3 — Channel sliders
```vue
<input type="range" min="0" max="255" v-model.number="rgb.r" />
```

### Step 4 — Preset swatches
```js
function applyPreset(h) {
  rgb.r = parseInt(h.slice(1, 3), 16);
  rgb.g = parseInt(h.slice(3, 5), 16);
  rgb.b = parseInt(h.slice(5, 7), 16);
}
```

**Edge cases**

| Case | Handling |
| :-- | :-- |
| Single-digit channel | `padStart(2, '0')`. |
| Invalid hex input | Validate before parsing. |
| Label contrast | Choose text color by luminance. |

**Interview tip:** `v-model.number` on the range inputs keeps channels numeric, and a `computed` hex means the preview and channels can never disagree — one canonical model, derived views.
